task_objective: Sync the PyPI project description to the GitHub README.
root_cause: [project] in pyproject.toml had neither a 'readme' nor 'description' field, so the built package metadata carried no long description and PyPI rendered nothing.
design_decisions: Added readme = "README.md" so Hatchling embeds the README as a text/markdown long description; uv build drives the same backend, so the release workflow is unchanged.
Added a description (Summary) one-liner sourced from the README intro.
No new tests: change is package metadata only. Verified by building the wheel and inspecting METADATA (Description-Content-Type text/markdown, Summary set, 3059-byte body matching README.md).
results: Build metadata now carries the full README. Pre-commit suite passed (ruff, ty, 55 tests). Committed on branch fix/pypi-readme-metadata. PyPI only refreshes the description on a new upload, so a version bump + tag is required to publish.
notes: README image links use absolute raw.githubusercontent.com URLs, so they render on PyPI.
One relative link remains: [RELEASING.md](docs/RELEASING.md) will not resolve on the PyPI page (minor).
The /tmp venv was stale; ./run.sh uv sync --all-extras --dev was needed before ty/pytest passed.
